channel.title: Muses Exchange — News
channel.description: Market commentary, data deep-dives, and field notes from the exchange for culture.

item.title: Latin sector quietly leads the exchange — here's the data
item.description: With 15 listed artists and an average price near $64, Latin is the second-most valuable sector on Muses Exchange. Bad Bunny, Karol G, and Feid are driving the numbers.

item.title: Catalog vs. Momentum — The Two Ways Artists Make Money on Streaming
item.description: Some artists earn from a 20-year-old back catalog. Others earn entirely on this month's release. The streaming economy splits into two business models — and they look completely different on a Muses chart.

item.title: Chappell Roan Went From $0.20 to $1.10 in 18 Months — Here's Why
item.description: The textbook viral-artist trajectory. Chappell Roan's Muses price 5x'd in 18 months on the back of a Tiny Desk performance, a festival run, and a single Spotify playlist add. Here's the data behind the climb.

item.title: The Five Signals That Move an Artist's Price the Most
item.description: Streams, velocity, YouTube, playlist position, social momentum — ranked by how much they actually move the price on Muses Exchange. The data behind every chart move.

item.title: $10,000 in Virtual Credits — What to Do With Them On Day One
item.description: A new Muses Exchange account starts with $10,000 in virtual credits. Here's how to actually use them — three opening strategies, what most beginners get wrong, and the one trade that teaches you the platform fastest.

item.title: Why Drake Is in a Different Price Scale Than Everyone Else
item.description: Drake sits at the top of Muses Exchange in a price band almost nobody else can reach. Here's why the platform uses log-compression to keep the chart readable — and what it means for trading him.

item.title: How Spotify Streams Translate to an Artist's Value
item.description: Streams are the new currency of the music industry, but they don't translate to artist value in a straight line. Here's how Spotify numbers become a price — and why raw stream counts are misleading.

item.title: What Is a Stock Market for Artists? A Beginner's Guide
item.description: A stock market for artists is a platform where musicians have prices that move with their real streaming data. Here's how it works, what makes it different from real investing, and why people are paying attention.
